text.title: Type Narrowing Patterns
text.summary: Type guards, discriminated unions, in/instanceof narrowing, and assertion functions for safe type refinement.

rule.statement: Model sum types as discriminated unions with a literal discriminant field; use that field to narrow instead of type assertions.
rule.rationale: Discriminated unions give exhaustiveness checking; type assertions bypass the type system and hide errors.

rule.statement: Use the in operator to narrow between types that share a union but differ by property presence; use instanceof for class hierarchies.
rule.rationale: These are the safe built-in narrowing forms — no risk of false narrowing that a manual cast would allow.

antipattern.description: Using the non-null assertion chain: user!.name!.first!
antipattern.reason: Every ! silently produces a TypeError at runtime if the value is actually null; use optional chaining and nullish coalescing instead.

antipattern.description: Casting unknown external data with "as User".
antipattern.reason: Type assertions are unchecked — invalid shapes pass at compile time but crash at runtime. Validate with a type guard or Zod instead.
